articles.title: ADOBE PUT AN AI COWORKER FOR YOUR EDITS IN PHOTOSHOP, EXPRESS, AND EVEN ACROBAT READER
articles.summary: Adobe is integrating AI "coworkers" into Photoshop, Express, and Acrobat that allow users to make edits using plain text descriptions instead of navigating traditional menus and tools. The company is also previewing Project Moonlight, which works across apps and can recognize user styles to help move from concept to finished work more seamlessly. All these features are built into Adobe Firefly, which combines generation, editing, and access to over 30 AI models in a single environment.

articles.title: IOS 26.4 OFFERS TWO LIQUID GLASS CUSTOMIZATION UPDATES
articles.summary: iOS 26.4 introduces expanded customization for the Liquid Glass interface with a new “Reduce Bright Effects” setting that minimizes flashing highlights during interactions, alongside an improved “Reduce Motion” feature that more reliably reduces animations for motion-sensitive users. Both options are found in Accessibility settings and continue Apple's incremental approach to UI customization, with rumors suggesting a more advanced slider control may arrive in iOS 27.

articles.title: YOUTUBE'S BRANDCONNECT HUB IS EVOLVING INTO A STREAMLINED CREATOR PARTNERSHIPS PLATFORM
articles.summary: YouTube has rebranded its BrandConnect creator marketing platform as YouTube Creator Partnerships, integrating Google's Gemini AI to help brands navigate the Partner Program's three million creators. The revamped hub also includes an updated version of Partnership Ads, which reportedly delivers an average 30% conversion lift by repurposing creator content as Shorts and in-stream ads. Announced at NewFronts, the move reflects YouTube's push to bring more precision and measurement tools to influencer-driven campaigns.

articles.title: DESIGN PATTERNS FOR BUILDING TRUST
articles.summary: Contrary to common assumptions, the safest path to user trust in AI isn't maximum transparency, but calibrated trust. Both aversion and overreliance are failure states. Transparency only aids calibration when users can actually verify system output. Design patterns such as scoped prompting, manual overrides, and AI disclosure help build trust across five psychological foundations.

articles.title: DESIGN'S INFLUENCE IS EXPANDING, AND HERE'S WHY THAT FEELS HARD
articles.summary: Design roles are expanding their influence across product development, blurring traditional boundaries between different team functions. The expansion of design's scope is creating challenges as role definitions become less clear.

articles.title: HOW USER CENTERED DESIGN BECAME A HANDBRAKE
articles.summary: UX evolved from a highly valued, insight-driven discipline into a more commoditized and siloed practice, where excessive specialization and rigid processes have reduced its impact and created friction with other roles. With pressures from AI, changing product practices, and growing independence of developers and product managers, UX is increasingly seen as an overhead rather than a driver of value. The field must adapt by broadening skills, focusing on outcomes and speed, and returning to its core strength of listening—or risk losing relevance.

articles.title: GENERATE SLIDES IN SECONDS
articles.summary: Deckary is a PowerPoint add-in that uses AI to help consulting and industry professionals build presentation slides in seconds.

articles.title: AI LOGO GENERATOR AND BRAND BUILDER
articles.summary: Design Rails is an AI-powered logo generator and brand builder that creates complete brand identities, including logos, colors, typography, and code-ready specifications in minutes through chat interaction.

articles.title: DESIGNERS ARE THE MOST UNDERRATED PEOPLE IN TECH
LOVART AI DESIGN AGENT AUTOMATES YOUR ENTIRE CREATIVE WORKFLOW
articles.summary: Lovart AI is the world's first autonomous creative platform that transforms single prompts into complete brand campaigns, app UIs, and design systems through its MCoT reasoning engine. The platform eliminates the need for designers to juggle multiple tools by handling planning and execution in a single integrated workflow. Since launching with a free tier, Lovart has attracted 100,000 users from 70 countries within five days, enabling designers to create brand systems that traditionally take months to build in just hours.

articles.title: IS IT ETHICAL TO PRESENT CONCEPT WORK AS IF IT'S REAL?
articles.summary: Concept design work is widely seen as valuable—especially for early-career designers—as it helps showcase creativity and potential when real client opportunities are limited. However, it differs from real-world projects, which involve client constraints, collaboration, and practical challenges, and becomes unethical when presented as if it were commissioned work. The consensus is that concept work should be clearly labelled, allowing designers to use it honestly while demonstrating their skills, since a strong portfolio reflects both creative ideas and real-world experience.

articles.title: FROM JAGUAR TO HBO: SHOULD BRANDS COMMIT TO A FAILED REBRAND?
articles.summary: Companies increasingly face backlash when implementing minimalist rebrands, creating a dilemma of whether to persist or revert to previous designs.

articles.title: FROM SYMBOLS TO SYSTEMS: DESIGNING BRANDS THAT HOLD UNDER PRESSURE
articles.summary: Resilient brand systems aren't built on exhaustive rules.
